findings.evidence: Line 32 has `@[expose, implicit_reducible]`; lines 38-45 already expose the characteristic set iff and `shiftInvariantSigma_le`.
findings.fix: Remove `@[expose]`; if unfolding is needed, add a named `shiftInvariantSigma_def` theorem instead of making the definition reducible to consumers.
findings.issue: `shiftInvariantSigma` is marked `@[expose]`, exposing the body `MeasurableSpace.invariants (shift α)` even though the file provides containment and measurable-set API.

findings.evidence: Roadmap lines 372-377 list `isShiftInvariant`, `shiftInvariantSigma`, `shiftInvariantSigma_le`, `mem_shiftInvariantSigma_iff`, `shiftInvariantSigma_measurable_shift_eq`, and `shiftInvariant_implies_shiftInvariantMeasurable`; `rg` finds only `measurableSet_shiftInvariantSigma_iff`, `measurable_shift_shiftInvariantSigma`, and `comp_shift_eq_of_measurable_shiftInvariantSigma` in this PR.
findings.fix: Rename or add aliases with the roadmap names, preserving the existing theorem bodies if desired.
findings.issue: The characteristic API is published under different names from the roadmap target, so downstream code looking for `mem_shiftInvariantSigma_iff`, `shiftInvariantSigma_measurable_shift_eq`, or `shiftInvariant_implies_shiftInvariantMeasurable` cannot use the promised interface.

findings.evidence: Line 38 proves `MeasurableSet[shiftInvariantSigma α] s ↔ MeasurableSet s ∧ IsShiftInvariant s` with no attribute; the public-API rubric requires normal-form characteristic lemmas to be annotated.
findings.fix: Put `@[simp]` on the final `mem_shiftInvariantSigma_iff`/`measurableSet_shiftInvariantSigma_iff` characteristic lemma, or provide a simp-normal-form alias with that statement.
findings.issue: The main measurable-set characterization lacks a normal-form automation annotation, so `simp` will not reduce `MeasurableSet[shiftInvariantSigma α] s` to ambient measurability plus shift invariance.

summary: The adapter exposes the definition body even though it provides characteristic lemmas, and it misses the public names/automation that the roadmap target says downstream code should consume.
